meta.name: Open Redirect
meta.description: Open redirect and URL redirection bypass payloads. Covers basic redirects, host validation bypasses, whitelist evasion, URL tricks, and header-based injection techniques.

payloads.description: Protocol-relative URL — browser supplies current protocol (http/https)
Absolute redirect — basic open redirect if no validation is applied
HTTP absolute redirect
Path confusion after redirect host — evades some suffix-based validators
Triple-slash redirect — some parsers treat as //attacker.com
Four-slash redirect — additional slash bypass variant
Backslash in URL — IE/Edge may interpret /\ as //
Double backslash — Windows-style UNC that some parsers follow
Dot URL-encoded in host — %2e=. to confuse host validators
Tab-delimited path bypass — %09 between slashes
URL credentials bypass — trusted.com treated as username; attacker.com is the host
Subdomain prefix confusion — trusted.com. is a subdomain of attacker.com
Path-based whitelist bypass — trusted.com appears in path, not host
URL-encoded slash separating domains — tricks host parsers
Encoded backslash between domains — %5c=\ treated as path separator in some parsers
Fragment bypass — trusted.com appears after # which is client-only and ignored server-side
Query param bypass — trusted.com in query, host is attacker.com
JavaScript scheme redirect — XSS if the redirect target is reflected into an href/location
Data URI redirect — executes JS that navigates to attacker site
CRLF + Location header injection — injects redirect header via CRLF in a parameter
URL-encoded CRLF Location injection — %0d=CR %0a=LF for header injection
Unicode CRLF injection — U+560A and U+560D normalize to \r\n in some servers
